command: for d in packages/*/; do echo "$(basename $d)"; done | sort
reason: List package names via bash loop

command: for pkg in enterprise console stats; do echo "=== packages/$pkg ===" && ls packages/$pkg/package.json 2>/dev/null && grep -c "test" packages/$pkg/package.json 2>/dev/null || echo "no package.json"; do
reason: Check enterprise, console, stats package.json for test scripts

command: for d in packages/*/; do pkg=$(basename $d); echo "=== $pkg ===" && [ -f "$d/package.json" ] && cat "$d/package.json" | grep -E '"name"|"test"' || echo "no package.json"; done
reason: Get package names and test scripts for every package

command: for d in packages/console/*/; do pkg=$(basename $d); echo "=== console/$pkg ===" && [ -f "$d/package.json" ] && cat "$d/package.json" | grep -E '"name"|"test"' || echo "no package.json"; done
reason: Check console sub-packages for package.json and test scripts

command: for f in .github/workflows/*.yml; do echo "--- $(basename $f) ---"; head -40 "$f"; echo; done
reason: Read first lines of all CI workflow files

command: for f in .github/workflows/*.yml; do basename "$f"; done | sort
